learning_outcomes.description: Explain the role of health behaviours in health promotion, including the prevention of chronic disease and injury prevention and management
Describe the main patterns of prevalence, social distribution and trends in health behaviours and explain the relevance of such patterns for health promotion practice
Critically appraise the quality of behavioural measurement methods for supporting health promotion strategies
Critically analyse published research on health behaviours and factors that influence health behaviours, with consideration of health promotion theory
Synthesise research on health behaviours and the influences of health behaviours to form a persuasive rationale for health promotion practice
Demonstrate effective written, visual and oral communication skills in regard to health promotion

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 1 - Health Behaviour Patterns & Measurement - In-Class Quizzes
assessments.task_description:
Two in-class quizzes will be held in the tutorials, the first in Week 5 and the second in Week 6. These quizzes will assess your applied understanding of concepts covered in lectures from the preceding weeks. The first quiz will test your critical understanding of the quality of behavioural measurement methods for supporting health promotion strategies.The second will test your understanding of the patterns of prevalence, social distribution and trends in health behaviours. Each quiz is worth 15% of your total course mark.

Specific instructions for this assessment task will be available in the Blackboard course site under 'Assessment' and the assessment will be discussed in the preceding lectures.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 2 - Behaviour Change Theory - Video Presentation
assessments.task_description:
You have been accepted as an intern with Queensland Health. Your supervisor has asked you to develop and deliver a presentation in which you will critically review current evidence on applying behaviour change theory to a key health behaviour. In your recorded video presentation, you will discuss aspects of the health behaviour for a specific population group, and critique the value of your chosen behaviour change theory for guiding the development of a behaviour change campaign.

Specific instructions for this assessment task are available in the Blackboard course site under 'Assessment'. This includes a marking criteria sheet. The assessment will be discussed in class.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You have been accepted as a health expert for an agency and your supervisor has asked you to prepare a report comparing and contrasting two mobile apps you have trialled through the semester. The report will cover a description of the health behaviour and audience, strengths and weaknesses of the apps for measuring health behaviour, and your recommendations for app development with regard to measurement issues. 

Specific instructions on this assessment are available in the Blackboard course site under 'Assessment'. Details on the task will also provided in the Week 1 Lecture and in the Week 2 workshop.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
